generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot"
]

instance GearwheelActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Intermediate gearwheel component in the gear transmission system of the robot's first axis"
]

instance CycloidalGearboxActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox reducing speed and increasing torque for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft providing real-time angular position measurements for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance GearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder positioned behind the gearbox providing angular position measurements of the output shaft"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionData
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor phase currents (I_M) via Siemens SINUMERIK 840D controller interface"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data - Engineering Models
instance MotorGearboxBDD : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing structural components (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox) and their rotational/electrical interconnections for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingFramework
]

instance MotorGearboxIBD : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) detailing internal structure and connections of the motor-gearbox subsystem for the first axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingFramework
]

// C10: Models/Data - Simulation Models
instance IdealSystemSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating ideal system behavior (without friction) for the motor-gearbox subsystem of first axis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingFramework
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction behavior for the gear transmission system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingFramework
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model implementing Stribeck friction model to simulate velocity-dependent friction losses"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingFramework
]

// C10: Data Traces
instance MeasuredMotorCurrentTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time motor current data stream (I_M) measured via Siemens SINUMERIK controller and encoder-derived torque calculations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisDashboard
]

instance MeasuredOutputAngleTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time output angle data trace derived from motor encoder and gearbox encoder measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionData, GearboxPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisDashboard
]

instance DerivedMotorTorqueTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Torque (τ_M) derived from motor encoder readings and manufacturer-provided dynamics model"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisDashboard
]

instance SimulatedMotorTorqueTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Torque trace (τ̇_M) simulated using Coulomb-Viscous friction model applied to motor dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData IdealSystemSimulation
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisDashboard
]

instance SimulatedGearwheelTorqueTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Torque trace (τ̇_G) simulated for gearwheel output using friction models applied to transmission system"
    DTDFVocab:fromData IdealSystemSimulation, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisDashboard
]

instance SimulatedSystemTorqueTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Overall system torque trace (τ_out) combining simulated motor and gearbox outputs with friction losses"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedMotorTorqueTrace, SimulatedGearwheelTorqueTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisDashboard
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for streaming real-time sensor data (motor current, encoder readings) from physical system to digital shadow"
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink environment hosting executable friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) for system simulation"
]

instance ProcessingFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow Reference Model Processing component handling data aggregation, model execution, and traceability for friction analysis"
]

instance FrictionAnalysisDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard visualizing real-time friction analysis by comparing measured vs. simulated data traces (motor output, gearwheel output, system output)"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeFrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous identification and refinement of friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) using live sensor data from motor-gearbox subsystem"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionModelComparisonInsight, AdaptiveControlUpdateAction
]

instance IntegratedVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dashboard integration for comparing measured vs. simulated data traces (motor output, gearwheel output, system output) to detect friction-related deviations"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight, MotionAccuracyOptimization
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionModelComparisonInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time comparison between measured friction characteristics (motor current, encoder readings) and simulated friction models to identify deviations"
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of friction behavior through dashboard showing measured vs. simulated torque/velocity traces for motor, gearwheel, and system outputs"
]

instance AdaptiveControlUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of friction parameters in control algorithm to compensate for wear, tool changes, or environmental factors (temperature)"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MotionAccuracyOptimization : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimization of motion accuracy by continuously refining friction models based on operational data"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance RobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearwheelActuator, CycloidalGearboxActuator,
    MotorEncoder, GearboxEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance DigitalShadowProcessingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MQTTProtocol, MATLABSimulinkEnvironment, ProcessingFramework,
    FrictionAnalysisDashboard, IdealSystemSimulation, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates in real-time, with data acquisition and processing synchronized to the operational cycle of the robot. The twinning process involves continuous, multi-physics-aware identification of friction models at the rate of sensor data streams (e.g., motor current and encoder readings), enabling adaptive control updates during operation. The time-scale is aligned with the robot’s operational frequency (e.g., 75°/s maximum speed), ensuring low-latency feedback for friction compensation."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation consists of a single, purpose-driven digital shadow for the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot. The constellation is centralized, with the digital shadow acting as a real-time data aggregation and analysis layer. It integrates physical sensors (encoders, motor current), simulation models (friction and dynamics), and a processing component to derive friction-related insights. The DT is not connected to other twins or external systems in this document."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin covers the operational lifecycle stage of the physical system, specifically the 'as-operated' phase. The digital shadow is designed to provide real-time insights into friction behavior during robot operation, enabling continuous model refinement and accuracy improvements. It does not explicitly address design or manufacturing stages, though the SysML-based structural model describes the physical asset's architecture."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Traceability**: Data traces are linked to their sources (e.g., encoders, simulation models) and metadata (e.g., time, parameters) via the Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM).
  - **Model Validation**: Simulation models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous friction) are validated against measured data to detect friction losses and deviations.
  - **Uncertainty Handling**: The use of two encoders per axis reduces positional uncertainty by comparing motor and gearbox encoder readings to account for elasticity and friction.
  - **Verification**: The digital shadow’s purpose-driven design ensures that data and models are aligned with the goal of improving motion accuracy by identifying friction-related errors."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s technical connection relies on:
  - **Data Transmission**: MQTT protocol for streaming sensor data (motor current, encoder readings) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D CNC controller to the digital shadow.
  - **Model Integration**: Asset Administration Shells (AAS) and SysML-based engineering models describe the robot’s structure and behavior, enabling seamless data linkage.
  - **Simulation Integration**: MathWorks MATLAB Simulink models are executed within the digital shadow to simulate friction effects and compare them with measured data."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is deployed as a **real-time system**:
  - **Hosting**: Likely integrated within the robot’s CNC controller (Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl) or a dedicated cloud/server infrastructure for data processing and visualization,
  - **Technology**: Combines embedded systems (for sensor data) and cloud-based tools (for simulation and dashboard)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to:
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: A UML-based reference model for defining data traces, models, and processing components (Fig. 1).
  - **SysML**: Standard modeling language for describing the robot’s structural and behavioral architecture (BDD/IBD).
  - **MQTT Protocol**: Standardized messaging protocol for real-time data streaming from sensors to the digital shadow."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  - **Real-time monitoring**: Dashboard provides visibility into system health to prevent accidents,
  - **Autonomous adjustments**: Digital twin can dynamically adjust control parameters to mitigate friction-induced errors,
  - **Remote operation**: Potential for remote monitoring/control of the robot (though not explicitly detailed in the document)."
]
